name: Solid Shapes — Faces, Edges and Corners
whatIsIt: Flat shapes are 2D; SOLID shapes are 3D — you can hold them. A CUBE (dice) and CUBOID (brick) have flat faces, straight edges and pointy corners (vertices). A SPHERE (ball) is fully round. A CYLINDER (tin) and CONE (party hat) mix flat and curved surfaces. We describe solids by counting faces, edges and vertices.
whyNeeded: The real world is 3D — boxes, balls, tins, ice-cream cones. Knowing faces, edges and corners helps you describe objects, build models, and later understand surface area and volume.
realLifeUses: A dice and a Rubik's cube are cubes
A brick and a matchbox are cuboids
A ball is a sphere; a tin is a cylinder
An ice-cream cone and a party hat are cones

vocabulary.meaning: A flat (or curved) surface of a solid.
The line where two faces meet.

story.title: The Toy Box Sort
story.text: Tara empties her toy box: a dice, a ball, a tin and a party hat. 'Sort them by how they roll or stack,' says Nana. 'The dice stacks — it has flat faces and corners. The ball rolls everywhere — it's round with no corners. The tin rolls on its side but stacks on its flat ends.' 'How many corners does the dice have?' asks Nana. Tara counts: 8.
story.extractedProblem: A dice (cube) — how many corners (vertices) does it have?

visual.caption: Solids around us: cube (dice), sphere (ball), cylinder (tin), cone (party hat).

teachingGallery.title: Count faces, edges, corners
teachingGallery.note: Cubes/cuboids: 6 faces, 12 edges, 8 corners. Round solids have curved surfaces.

examples.caption: A cube — all faces are equal squares.
A cylinder rolls on its side, stacks on its ends.

standardMethod.summary: Name a solid by its surfaces; describe it by counting faces, edges and vertices.
steps: Look at the surfaces: all flat (cube/cuboid), fully round (sphere), or a mix (cylinder/cone).
Count EDGES (where two faces meet) and VERTICES (pointy corners).
A cube and cuboid both have 6 faces, 12 edges, 8 vertices.

workedExamples.problem: How many faces, edges and vertices does a cube have?
A tin of food is which solid, and how many corners does it have?
steps: It has two flat circular ends and a curved side → cylinder.
A cylinder has no pointy corners.

commonMistakes.mistake: Calling a solid by its flat-face name, e.g. calling a cube a 'square'.
commonMistakes.fix: A square is flat (2D); a cube is solid (3D) with square FACES. Use the solid's own name.

commonMistakes.mistake: Mixing up faces, edges and vertices when counting.
commonMistakes.fix: FACE = a surface, EDGE = a line where two faces meet, VERTEX = a corner point. Count one type at a time.

easy.q: How many faces does a cube have?

medium.q: How many corners (vertices) does a cuboid have?
hintLadder: Count the pointy corners of a box.

medium.q: A party hat is which solid?
hintLadder: One round base and a point on top.

challenge.q: How many edges does a cube have?
hintLadder: Edges are lines where faces meet.

challenge.q: Rohan calls a dice a 'square'. What should he say?

options.label: A cube — its faces are squares

challenge.answer: A cube — its faces are squares
hintLadder: A dice is solid, not flat.

questions.q: A cuboid has how many faces?
A tin of food is a…
How many corners does a sphere have?
An EDGE of a solid is…

options.label: The line where two faces meet

questions.answer: The line where two faces meet

teachBackPrompt: Find four solid objects at home (a box, a ball, a tin, a cone-shaped thing). Name each solid and tell a grown-up its faces, edges and corners — and which ones roll, stack, or slide.

revisionCard.summary: Solids are 3D. Cube & cuboid: 6 faces, 12 edges, 8 corners. Sphere: round, 0 corners. Cylinder: 2 flat + 1 curved surface, 0 corners. Cone: 1 flat + 1 curved, 1 corner. Face = surface, edge = line, vertex = corner.
